medications.drug_name: systemic oral antifungal (griseofulvin or terbinafine — paediatric, weight-based)
medications.indication_rationale: MANDATORY for tinea capitis — topicals cannot reach the hair root; continue for ~6-8 weeks. Choice/dose per organism and paediatric guidance/weight.
medications.dose_route_frequency: weight-based paediatric dosing per prescriber; take griseofulvin with a fatty meal (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: short oral corticosteroid (e.g., prednisolone, weight-based) for the kerion
medications.indication_rationale: Rapidly reduces the severe inflammatory hypersensitivity of the kerion to limit pain and scarring alopecia; short course.
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g., ~1 mg/kg daily for ~5 days (as per source note; prescriber to confirm)

medications.indication_rationale: Reduces surface shedding/contagion (adjunct to systemic therapy).
A kerion is a fungal hypersensitivity mass, not a bacterial abscess — do not incise/squeeze it.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Don't squeeze/pick the lump; don't share hats/combs/pillows; have the new puppy checked by a vet for ringworm; take the antifungal daily (griseofulvin with a fatty meal) for the full course.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents scarring/spread, treats the animal source, and ensures cure.

patient_education_points.point: This is a severe scalp ringworm (fungal) infection with a 'kerion' — the big painful lump is your child's immune reaction to the fungus, not a boil. It must NOT be cut open; that would hurt and scar without helping.

Creams alone can't cure it — your child needs the antifungal tablets/liquid for 6–8 weeks (given daily with milk/fatty food), plus a short steroid course to settle the swelling and protect the hair roots.

Don't share hats/combs, get the puppy checked by a vet, and take your child to a paediatric emergency department if the redness spreads down the face/neck, he gets a high fever, or becomes very sleepy/confused.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Reduction of kerion inflammation, fungal cultures, and the long-term antifungal plan.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Spreading facial/neck redness, high fever, or lethargy/confusion — paediatric emergency.

must_include_items: Recognise a kerion (fungal hypersensitivity) — NOT a bacterial abscess — and do NOT incise/drain
Start SYSTEMIC oral antifungal (mandatory; weight-based) + a short oral steroid for the kerion
Antifungal shampoo + contagion/animal-source and hygiene measures
Secondary-infection/systemic safety-net; arrange fungal cultures/paediatric dosing
errors_of_omission: No steroid for the kerion (scarring risk)
errors_of_commission: Incising/draining the kerion (treating as an abscess)
Advising the parent to squeeze the lump
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires recognising the kerion (no I&D), SYSTEMIC antifungal + short steroid, contagion/animal measures, and a systemic safety-net. Advising I&D or topical-only therapy are key failures.
